text:
In this chapter:

(1) “Border” means the border between this state and the United Mexican States.

(2) “Border commercial zone” means a commercial zone established under 49 C.F.R. Part 372, Subpart B, any portion of which is contiguous to the border in this state.

(3) “Commercial motor vehicle” includes a foreign commercial motor vehicle.

(4) “Foreign commercial motor vehicle” means a commercial motor vehicle, as defined by 49 C.F.R. Section 390.5, that is owned by a person or entity that is domiciled in or a citizen of a country other than the United States.

(5) “Motor carrier” includes a foreign motor carrier and a foreign motor private carrier, as defined in 49 U.S.C. Sections 13102(6) and (7).
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 648. Foreign Commercial Motor Transp.
##### § 648.001 — Definitions

In this chapter:

    (1) “Border” means the border between this state and the United Mexican States.

    (2) “Border commercial zone” means a commercial zone established under 49 C.F.R. Part 372, Subpart B, any portion of which is contiguous to the border in this state.

    (3) “Commercial motor vehicle” includes a foreign commercial motor vehicle.

    (4) “Foreign commercial motor vehicle” means a commercial motor vehicle, as defined by 49 C.F.R. Section 390.5, that is owned by a person or entity that is domiciled in or a citizen of a country other than the United States.

    (5) “Motor carrier” includes a foreign motor carrier and a foreign motor private carrier, as defined in 49 U.S.C. Sections 13102(6) and (7).

*Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 39 (H.B.782), Sec. 1, eff. September 1, 2009.*

subsections.text: “Border” means the border between this state and the United Mexican States.
“Border commercial zone” means a commercial zone established under 49 C.F.R. Part 372, Subpart B, any portion of which is contiguous to the border in this state.
“Commercial motor vehicle” includes a foreign commercial motor vehicle.

“Foreign commercial motor vehicle” means a commercial motor vehicle, as defined by 49 C.F.R. Section 390.5, that is owned by a person or entity that is domiciled in or a citizen of a country other than the United States.
“Motor carrier” includes a foreign motor carrier and a foreign motor private carrier, as defined in 49 U.S.C. Sections 13102(6) and (7).
